seoTitle: How to Create Interactive Presentations With Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to build a clickable, hyperlink-driven presentation in Canva so viewers can navigate slides interactively during pitches or lessons.
intro: A presentation that lets viewers click between sections feels far more engaging than a straight linear slideshow. This guide covers setting up your presentation in Canva, adding hyperlinks between slides, and applying best practices for interactivity. It's aimed at educators and business professionals who want their presentations to hold attention better.

steps.heading: Set up your presentation
steps.body: Start your Canva presentation as you normally would, planning which sections you'll want viewers to jump between.

steps.heading: Add hyperlinks to your slides
steps.body: Insert hyperlinks on buttons, text, or images so clicking them takes viewers to a different slide or section.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for interactivity
steps.body: Keep navigation intuitive so viewers can move through the interactive presentation without confusion.

steps.heading: Explore real-world use cases
steps.body: Use this technique for client pitches, educational lessons, or any presentation where letting viewers choose their own path improves engagement.

faq.question: How do hyperlinks work in a Canva presentation?
faq.answer: Hyperlinks can be added to text, buttons, or images within a slide, letting viewers click to jump to a different slide or section.

faq.question: Who benefits from interactive presentations made in Canva?
faq.answer: Educators, business professionals, and anyone pitching to clients or teaching students can use hyperlinked presentations to improve engagement.

faq.question: Is creating a clickable presentation difficult for beginners?
faq.answer: No, this tutorial is designed to be beginner-friendly, ensuring even those with minimal design experience can follow along.

faq.question: Can I use Canva templates as a starting point for an interactive presentation?
faq.answer: Yes, using Canva's templates to streamline your workflow is part of the recommended approach before adding hyperlinks.
